title: Mirabilis grandiflora
intro: Mirabilis grandiflora, commonly known as the large-flowered four o'clock, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Four O'Clock family (Nyctaginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions, including wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Mirabilis grandiflora include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Herbal preparations made from Mirabilis grandiflora include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal benefits.

uses: Mirabilis grandiflora, also known as the snapgillyflower, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
